data.title: CHAPTER 1: WHAT CLAUDE CAN AND CANNOT DO IN EDUCATION
blocks: It is 9:30 on a Sunday evening. Dana teaches eighth-grade English in a large suburban middle school. She has a stack of
Chapter 1: What Claude Can and Cannot Do — She types: Help me grade these essays.

data.title: THE FOUR-TASK TAXONOMY.
blocks: · Generating five alternative practice problems for a concept already planned
· Drafting a list of common student misconceptions about a topic for the teacher to review
· Producing three different analogies for an abstract concept so the teacher can pick the one that fits the class
· Rewriting a passage at a lower reading level for a student who needs it
· Brainstorming discussion questions the teacher will curate

data.title: A WORKED TEACHING WALKTHROUGH: SORTING DANA'S TASKS
blocks: Grading thirty-one essays.
Lesson plan for the new unit.
Differentiated materials for three IEP students.

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — WHAT CLAUDE GETS WRONG IN EDUC

data.title: WHAT CLAUDE GETS WRONG IN EDUCATION — SPECIFICALLY
blocks: Overhelping students.
Generic feedback.
Hallucinated facts.
Standards confusion.
Confidently wrong rubrics.
data.section: WHAT CLAUDE GETS WRONG IN EDUC

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — THE HUMAN GATE QUESTIONS FOR T

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE QUESTIONS FOR THIS CHAPTER.
blocks: Which category does this task belong to assist, draft-with-review, protect, or policy-dependent?
If draft-with-review what specific professional knowledge must I apply to the review?
If protect am I substituting Claude output for my judgment, or am I using Claude to support my thinking before I make the judgment?
If policy-dependent have I confirmed my institution's current position on this task?
· What is the student-learning risk if this output is wrong or generic?
data.section: THE HUMAN GATE QUESTIONS FOR T

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Treating "draft-with-review" as "done."
Using Category 1 framing to justify Category 2 tasks.
Putting student-identifiable information into Claude as a matter of routine.
Assuming Claude knows your context.
Accepting confident output as accurate output.
